objective: Add spawn/kill buttons to the boot-waterfall UI. Subprocess lifecycle owned by topology-ui process. All actions emit spans.
in_scope: Add 'Spawn <type>' buttons (4 buttons: gateway/broker/compute/schema) to the UI
Add 'Kill' button per node in the dropdown list
POST /api/nodes/spawn { node_type } → forks subprocess with inherited OTEL_* env
DELETE /api/nodes/{node_name} → SIGTERM, then SIGKILL after 5s grace
The spawned subprocess's own boot-span chain shows up in the UI's waterfall view (closes the loop)
out_of_scope: Topology graph (no fleet visualization in v1)
Persistent data dirs (subprocesses are ephemeral; killing wipes their dir)
exit_criteria: Click 'Spawn gateway' in UI → subprocess starts within 5s, appears in node-selector dropdown
Click 'Kill' on any node → subprocess terminates within 10s, disappears from dropdown
Spawn 4 different types → all 4 visible in dropdown, can view boot waterfall for each
Spawned subprocess's boot waterfall queryable via UI (proves OTEL_* env propagation)
Jaeger URL pre-filtered to spawn → boot chain
discipline: Still no SPA framework (vanilla HTML+CSS+JS per Principle #10)
All config via env vars per Principle #8: RAFKAV2_UI_SPAWN_DIR (default E:/tmp/rafkav2-ui-nodes)
Closes with Jaeger URL per Principle #7
